text.title: Data Analysis Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end example: customer entity across CRM + billing.

section.title: Step 1 — inventory
section.code: sources: - id: crm type: postgres entities: [customers, contacts, accounts] - id: billing type: stripe-export entities: [customers, subscriptions, invoices]

section.title: Step 2 — normalize fields (customer entity)
section.code: | name | type | source_system | source_ref | nullable | pii | |-------------|----------|---------------|-----------------------|----------|-----| | customer_id | uuid | crm | crm.customers.id | false | no | | email | string | crm | crm.customers.email | false | yes | | stripe_id | string | billing | billing.customers.id | true | no | | created_at | datetime | crm | crm.customers.created | false | no |

section.title: Step 3 — owner
section.p: customer owner_name: Maria Lopes, owner_role: Data Steward (Revenue domain).

section.title: Step 4 — DQ baseline
section.code: | dimension | score | method | |---------------|-------|--------| | accuracy | 92 | CRM↔billing email match on 10k sample | | completeness | 88 | non-null ratio on required fields | | consistency | 75 | naming-convention check across sources | | timeliness | 95 | freshness window p95 < 60s | | validity | 91 | regex + enum checks | | uniqueness | 99 | customer_id distinct ratio |

section.title: Step 5 — business rules
section.code: | id | predicate | severity | |-------|-------------------------------------------------|----------| | br-01 | regex(email, '^[^@]+@[^@]+$') | block | | br-02 | stripe_id IS NULL OR stripe_id startswith 'cus_'| warn | | br-03 | created_at <= now() | block |

section.title: Step 6 — publish
section.p: version_tag v1.0.0 committed. Downstream pins to ^1.0 range.
